summary: The mismatches on walk_left and walk_right outputs are likely due to incorrect direction-flip logic in the state transitions for S_WALK_LEFT and S_WALK_RIGHT. The current RTL flips direction on either bump_left or bump_right in both states, but the correct behavior is to flip direction only when bumping into the wall in the current direction (i.e., bump_left while walking left, bump_right while walking right, or both bumps). The patch updates the state transition logic to only flip direction on the appropriate bump(s) for each walking state, matching the prompt's requirements.
risks: If the testbench or reference model interprets simultaneous bumps differently, further mismatches may occur.
If there are additional corner cases for bump handling not covered in the prompt, they may not be handled.
If the testbench expects direction to flip on both bumps regardless of state, this change may cause new mismatches.
next_steps: Re-run simulation to verify that walk_left and walk_right outputs now match the reference.
Check for any new mismatches or regressions in other outputs.
If mismatches persist, examine the testbench stimulus and reference model for further clues.
